question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: nodeMapDecodeHookFunc()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a NodeMap entry via HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES with a node account ID of '0.0.0' (zero account) to cause hiero.AccountIDFromString() to return a zero-value AccountID, which when used in hiero.ClientForNetworkV2() routes submitted transactions to an invalid node, causing all submissions to fail?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: nodeMapDecodeHookFunc()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a NodeMap entry via HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES with a node account ID using a non-standard shard/realm (e.g., '1.2.3') to cause hiero.ClientForNetworkV2() to build a client targeting a non-existent shard, causing all submitted transactions to be rejected by the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES to a value with extra colons in the node string (e.g., 'host:port:extra:accountId') to exploit the strings.Split(node, ':') logic — which expects exactly 3 parts — and trigger an 'Invalid node string' error that crashes LoadConfig(), preventing the server from starting and blocking all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: loadNodeMapFromEnv()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODES to a value where the port component is non-numeric (e.g., '192.168.0.1:abc:0.0.3') to cause fmt.Sprintf('%s:%s', parts[0], parts[1]) to construct a malformed endpoint key in the NodeMap, which when passed to hiero.ClientForNetworkV2() causes transaction submission to fail silently or route to an invalid endpoint?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit v.AutomaticEnv() to set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_HOST to an attacker-controlled database host, causing the Rosetta server to connect to a rogue database that returns falsified transaction history, making submitted transactions appear confirmed when they are not?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit v.AutomaticEnv() to set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_PORT to an attacker-controlled port, redirecting database connections to a rogue service that returns manipulated transaction records, causing the Rosetta API to report false transaction states after submission?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Db.GetDsn()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hardcoded 'sslmode=disable' in GetDsn() — combined with a DB host override via HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_HOST env var — to perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the unencrypted database connection, intercepting or modifying transaction records returned after submission?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Db.GetDsn()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that GetDsn() constructs the DSN with fmt.Sprintf and no sanitization of the Password field, to inject PostgreSQL DSN options (e.g., a password value containing ' sslmode=require host=attacker.com') that override the hardcoded sslmode=disable and redirect DB connections, tampering with transaction state lookups?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Db.GetDsn()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that GetDsn() constructs the DSN with fmt.Sprintf and no sanitization of the Username field, to inject PostgreSQL DSN options (e.g., a username value containing ' host=attacker.com') that redirect the database connection to an attacker-controlled host, allowing manipulation of transaction data returned after submission?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Db.GetDsn()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that GetDsn() constructs the DSN with fmt.Sprintf and no sanitization of the Host field, to inject PostgreSQL DSN options (e.g., a host value containing ' sslmode=require') that override the hardcoded sslmode=disable, altering the security posture of the database connection used to verify submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit v.AutomaticEnv() to set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_PASSWORD to a value containing DSN injection characters (e.g., spaces and key=value pairs), causing GetDsn() to produce a malformed or attacker-influenced DSN that redirects the database connection, tampering with transaction verification after submission?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit v.AutomaticEnv() to set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_RESPONSE_MAXTRANSACTIONSINBLOCK to 0 or a negative value, causing the block API to return empty transaction lists, making submitted transactions invisible to clients querying the block data and effectively hiding transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit v.AutomaticEnv() to set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_RESPONSE_MAXTRANSACTIONSINBLOCK to an extremely large value, causing the block API to attempt to load an unbounded number of transactions per block, exhausting memory and causing a denial-of-service that prevents transaction submission confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit v.AutomaticEnv() to set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_CACHE_ENTITY_MAXSIZE to 0, disabling the entity cache, causing every transaction submission that requires entity resolution to hit the database with no caching, enabling a cache-starvation denial-of-service that slows or blocks transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit v.AutomaticEnv() to set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_HTTP_READTIMEOUT to an extremely small value (e.g., '1ns'), causing the HTTP server to time out reading transaction submission requests before they are fully received, effectively blocking all ConstructionSubmit calls?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit v.AutomaticEnv() to set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_HTTP_WRITETIMEOUT to an extremely small value (e.g., '1ns'), causing the HTTP server to time out writing transaction submission responses before they are sent, leaving clients unable to determine if their transaction was submitted successfully?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit v.AutomaticEnv() to set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_HTTP_IDLETIMEOUT to '0', disabling idle connection timeouts, enabling a slowloris-style attack where an attacker holds open many idle connections to exhaust server resources and prevent legitimate transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit v.AutomaticEnv() to set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_SHUTDOWNTIMEOUT to '0', causing the server to shut down immediately without waiting for in-flight transaction submissions to complete, resulting in submitted transactions being lost or left in an unknown state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit v.AutomaticEnv() to set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_LOG_LEVEL to 'debug', causing the server to log full transaction details (including signed transaction bytes) to logs that may be accessible to other unprivileged users, enabling transaction replay or analysis attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit v.AutomaticEnv() to set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NODEVERSION to a crafted string that is included in the Rosetta /network/options response, causing clients to believe they are interacting with a different node version and potentially bypassing client-side version checks before submitting transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit v.AutomaticEnv() to set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_FEATURE_SUBNETWORKIDENTIFIER to 'true', causing the network identifier to include a SubNetworkIdentifier with shard/realm values from config, which if mismatched with what clients expect, causes transaction submissions to be rejected by the Rosetta asserter?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit v.AutomaticEnv() to set HIERO_MIRROR_COMMON_SHARD or HIERO_MIRROR_COMMON_REALM to unexpected values, causing the system entity resolution (used in transaction routing and account lookup) to reference wrong shard/realm entities, leading to incorrect transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit v.AutomaticEnv() to set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_STATEMENTTIMEOUT to 0, disabling database query timeouts, enabling an attacker to submit transactions that trigger slow database queries and hold connections indefinitely, exhausting the database connection pool and blocking other transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit v.AutomaticEnv() to set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_POOL_MAXOPENCONNECTIONS to 1, severely limiting database concurrency, causing transaction submission requests that require database lookups to queue and time out under moderate load, effectively blocking transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit v.AutomaticEnv() to set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_DB_POOL_MAXLIFETIME to 0, causing database connections to be recycled immediately after each use, creating excessive connection churn that degrades performance and causes transaction submission failures under load?
